ambiguities.aspect: Initial loan recognition at face value vs amortized cost
ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: Short-term loan with stated interest rate of 4.5% is recorded at face value ($2,000,000) as transaction costs are not mentioned and the term is only one year
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IFRS 9: short-term financial asset with stated interest rate is initially recognized at transaction price ($2,000,000) absent evidence of financing arrangement or transaction costs

ambiguities.aspect: Whether to accrue first month's interest at inception
ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: Transaction describes the loan advance only; interest is 'receivable at the end of each month' so no accrual is recorded at inception
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IFRS 9: interest revenue is recognized over time using the effective interest method, but at inception (day 0) no time has elapsed so no interest is accrued

ambiguities.aspect: Classification of loan as current vs non-current asset
ambiguities.input_contextualized_conventional_defaul: One-year term loan is a current asset (matures within 12 months)
ambiguities.input_contextualized_ifrs_default: IAS 1: asset expected to be realized within 12 months is classified as current

best_attempt.reason: Loan advanced from chequing account to business partner at face value

flags.aspect: Interest calculation and monthly accrual pattern
